reason: Response B stays disciplined and grounded in the actual auditory experience (sustained chord, stepwise solo voice, dissonances rubbing, the tide-not-wave swell) and earns an honest emotional ambivalence, whereas Response A overshoots into performed profundity with grandiose theses ("what serious music teaches and philosophy merely proposes"), strained geological/sculptural abstraction, and occasionally broken syntax ("everything resolving into consonance produces not rest but a stranger vertigo"). The piling-on of conceptual abstraction and the self-congratulatory register in A read like an imitator's idea of Opus prose rather than the controlled, sensory, cadence-aware writing I tend toward. Hence B is more likely genuinely mine, though A's fluency leaves real uncertainty.
